name: RAAS blocker + mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist hyperkalemia screen
risk: Additive reduction in potassium excretion can cause clinically important hyperkalemia, particularly with CKD, diabetes, dehydration, or concurrent NSAID use.
safety_action: Do not reassure or adjust therapy from a historical potassium alone; use current potassium and renal function and follow the local heart-failure/RAAS monitoring protocol.
urgency_changers: Potassium at or above the local emergency threshold, ECG changes, oliguria, or rapidly worsening kidney function.
pharmacist_traps: A previously tolerated combination can become unsafe during acute illness or after an NSAID is added.
